components.title: Power Generator (Backup Diesel Generator)
components.description: Diesel backup generator providing emergency power to the Main Office Server Room and associated building critical loads in the event of a mains power outage, automatically starting via an automatic transfer switch (ATS) within seconds of a detected outage. Sized to run the full server room load (including precision air conditioning) plus building life-safety systems. Fuel supply is maintained for a documented minimum runtime, and the generator is load-tested on a scheduled basis.
components.purpose: Maintain power availability to the Main Office Server Room and critical building systems during a mains power outage, bridging the gap until mains power is restored or the site's UPS capacity would otherwise be exhausted.

props.name: location
props.value: External generator enclosure, main office site

props.name: capacity
props.value: Sized for full server room load plus precision air conditioning and building life-safety systems

props.name: transfer-mechanism
props.value: Automatic Transfer Switch (ATS) — automatic start and transfer within seconds of detected mains outage

props.name: fuel-runtime
props.value: On-site diesel fuel maintained for a documented minimum runtime; refuelling arrangement in place for extended outages

props.name: testing
props.value: Scheduled load-testing under simulated outage conditions

control-implementations.description: Control implementations for the Power Generator (Backup Diesel Generator).

implemented-requirements.description: The Power Generator, together with the site's Uninterruptible Power Supply, provides a resilient power feed to the IT equipment in the Main Office Server Room, ensuring continuity of power during a mains outage until either mains power is restored or the outage is resolved.

The Power Generator's capacity, fuel runtime, and scheduled load-testing regime form part of the organisation's business continuity and disaster recovery planning, addressing extended mains power loss as a business continuity risk to IT service availability.

The Power Generator supports the continued operation of the Main Office Server Room as a suitably secure, continuously available facility for the organisation's non-classified IT equipment during a power outage.

Vendor technicians servicing or refuelling the Power Generator are escorted by an authorised staff member where the generator enclosure is located within, or provides access to, a physically secured area.
